body_txt: Ovivory is when animals eat others' eggs. While humans do this all the time with poultry (albeit usually taking eggs that are unfertilized, meaning they can't hatch into anything), non-human animals who do so tend to be depicted as cowardly , awful thieves that eat the eggs of innocent animals; it's usually a bird, though reptiles like crocodiles, (non-avian) dinosaurs, and (in fantasy works) dragons and griffins can also be victims. note Make what you will that these are all creatures who lay eggs of the hard mineralized shell kind, it's fairly rare for most soft-egg layers like arthropods and amphibians to be on the end of it. It's often a foe of the Egg MacGuffin the heroes need to guard against, who don't necessarily need to be its parents who are absent for whatever reason. The angry mother or father ( or both , frequently at once ) will instead often show up at the climax and terrify the thief into fleeing. These egg-eaters will usually be depicted as incredibly thin , filthy, and even malodorous, stand with a hunched-over posture, and slink around like there's no tomorrow. In many cases, they're merely comic relief minions of the Big Bad , often in duos or trios . More serious cases will have lots of them Zerg Rush the nest and try to overwhelm the defenders. Species that routinely get this treatment include: This trope often overlaps with Scavengers Are Scum , another trope about ecological niches unfairly demonized in popular culture. The fact that many species associated with one also do the other may or may not help. If a human or humanoid being does it, this usually falls under Evil Poacher ( not like the cooking kind ), though they can still have the traits mentioned here. Attempts to devour the seeds of sentient or otherwise-precious plants might qualify as a variant, if the would-be eater is sufficiently villainous. Finally, compare Would Hurt a Child , Offing the Offspring , and Eats Babies for when after the eggs hatch. NOTE: A creature seen hunting eggs can't qualify for this trope alone. It has to be depicted and treated as cowardly and evil for it. No Real Life Examples, Please! Animals are not inherently evil in Real Life .
